title: Discovery Service: Database Schema and Alembic Migrations (PostgreSQL + SQLAlchemy 2.0 async)
description: Define all database tables for the Discovery Service using SQLAlchemy 2.0 declarative async ORM models. Create the complete schema covering: applications, services, sentinel identities, sentinel instances, enrollment tokens (one-time), VC credential metadata, status lists, revocation events, config bundles, audit events (append-only), chain cache tables, and policy snapshots. Set up Alembic for versioned migrations.
objectives: Robust and auditable persistence for the complete sentinel/VC lifecycle
Idempotent and concurrent-safe one-time token consumption
Append-only audit_events table — no DELETE or UPDATE permitted at application layer

steps: Step 1 - Base Setup: Use SQLAlchemy 2.0 async with asyncpg driver. Create services/discovery/src/discovery/db/base.py with DeclarativeBase class. Create db/session.py with async_engine (create_async_engine, pool_size=10, max_overflow=20) and AsyncSessionFactory. All models inherit from Base.

Step 2 - apps Table: id (UUID PK), name (TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL), owner (TEXT), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ default now()), updated_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), is_active (BOOL default true). Represents top-level application entities.

Step 3 - services Table: id (UUID PK), app_id (UUID FK → apps.id), service_id (TEXT NOT NULL), env (TEXT NOT NULL CHECK env IN ('dev','test','prod')), display_name (TEXT), description (TEXT), owner_did (TEXT), is_active (BOOL default true), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), updated_at (TIMESTAMPTZ). UNIQUE(service_id, env). Index on (service_id, env, is_active).

Step 4 - sentinels Table: id (UUID PK), service_id (UUID FK → services.id), did (TEXT NOT NULL), role (TEXT NOT NULL CHECK role IN ('producer','consumer')), env (TEXT NOT NULL), is_active (BOOL default true), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), last_seen (TIMESTAMPTZ), config_version (INT default 0). UNIQUE(did, role, env).

Step 5 - sentinel_instances Table: id (UUID PK), sentinel_id (UUID FK → sentinels.id), instance_id (TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE), base_url (TEXT), status (TEXT CHECK status IN ('active','draining','offline')), last_seen (TIMESTAMPTZ), metadata (JSONB). Index on (sentinel_id, status).

Step 6 - enrollment_tokens Table: id (UUID PK), jti (TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE), service_id (TEXT NOT NULL), role (TEXT NOT NULL), env (TEXT NOT NULL), status (TEXT NOT NULL CHECK status IN ('PENDING','APPROVED','CONSUMED','EXPIRED')), created_by (TEXT), approved_by (TEXT), token_hash (TEXT NOT NULL COMMENT 'SHA-256 of the JWT — raw token never stored'), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), approved_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), expires_at (TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL), consumed_at (TIMESTAMPTZ). Index on (jti), Index on (status, expires_at).

Step 7 - credentials Table: id (UUID PK), credential_type (TEXT NOT NULL), issuer_did (TEXT NOT NULL), subject_did (TEXT NOT NULL), audience (TEXT), env (TEXT), jti (TEXT UNIQUE), issued_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), expires_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), status (TEXT NOT NULL CHECK status IN ('active','revoked','expired','deprecated')), status_list_id (TEXT), status_list_index (INT), is_latest (BOOL default true). Index on (subject_did, env, is_latest). NEVER store VC JWT plaintext — store only metadata.

Step 8 - status_lists Table: id (UUID PK), status_list_id (TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE), issuer_did (TEXT NOT NULL), env (TEXT NOT NULL), credential_type (TEXT), current_hash (TEXT NOT NULL), bitstring_url (TEXT), published_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), version (INT default 1), anchor_tx_hash (TEXT), anchor_block (BIGINT), anchor_pending (BOOL default true). Index on (status_list_id, env).

Step 9 - audit_events Table: id (UUID PK), event_id (TEXT UNIQUE), ts (TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL default now()), actor_type (TEXT NOT NULL), actor_id (TEXT), action (TEXT NOT NULL), target_type (TEXT), target_id (TEXT), summary (TEXT), request_id (TEXT), ip_address (TEXT), prev_hash (TEXT), event_hash (TEXT NOT NULL). APPEND-ONLY: no UPDATE or DELETE via ORM. Partition by month in PostgreSQL for performance. Index on (ts, actor_id, action).

Step 10 - config_bundles Table: id (UUID PK), sentinel_id (UUID FK → sentinels.id), version (INT NOT NULL), bundle_hash (TEXT), signed_bundle_jws (TEXT NOT NULL), issued_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), is_current (BOOL default true). Index on (sentinel_id, is_current).

Step 11 - Alembic Setup: alembic init in services/discovery/. alembic.ini: sqlalchemy.url = ${DATABASE_URL}. env.py: target_metadata = Base.metadata, run_async_migrations = True. Create initial migration: alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'initial_schema'. Seed migration for dev: inserts admin user, test app/service records.

Step 12 - Repository Layer: For each table, create a repository class in services/discovery/src/discovery/repositories/. Example: EnrollmentTokenRepository.consume_atomic(session, jti) → uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED to atomically commit a token. Returns the token record or raises TokenAlreadyConsumedError.

critical_constraints: UNIQUE(service_id, env) on services table prevents duplicate service registrations
UNIQUE(did, role, env) on sentinels prevents identity collision
UNIQUE(jti) on enrollment_tokens provides replay protection
enrollment_tokens.consumed_at is set atomically via SELECT FOR UPDATE — only one concurrent consumer wins
audit_events has no application-level DELETE/UPDATE — enforced via ORM-layer restriction
indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_services_service_env ON services(service_id, env, is_active)
CREATE INDEX idx_enrollment_status_exp ON enrollment_tokens(status, expires_at)
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_ts ON audit_events(ts DESC)
CREATE INDEX idx_audit_actor ON audit_events(actor_id, action, ts DESC)

repository_exceptions: TokenNotFoundError: enrollment token JTI does not exist
TokenAlreadyConsumedError: token consumed_at is already set

security_constraints: VC JWT payload MUST NOT be stored in the database — only metadata (jti, type, issuer, subject, exp, status)
enrollment_tokens MUST store only SHA-256(token_jwt) — never the raw JWT
audit_events MUST have no UPDATE or DELETE route in any repository or router
DB connection string MUST use SecretStr — never logged
asyncpg connection pool recycled after 3600s to prevent stale connections

acceptance_criteria: alembic upgrade head creates all tables on a blank PostgreSQL database
UNIQUE constraints are enforced (duplicate service_id+env raises IntegrityError)
consume_atomic() is safe under concurrent test (two requests for same token: one succeeds, one raises TokenAlreadyConsumedError)
No application code path can UPDATE or DELETE from audit_events
alembic downgrade base succeeds without errors
